profile.name: Jennifer Brown
profile.role_in_household: Primary client; tends to lead financial discussions and bring documents/figures
profile.financial_literacy_level: High — long comfort with markets, taxes, and account types; asks pointed questions and notices fee/tax details
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but optimization-driven; willing to take market risk if there is a clear rationale and tax efficiency, gets uneasy with vague or story-based investments
profile.communication_style: Direct and structured; speaks in bullet points, occasionally interrupts to correct details; uses phrases like "I think" and "if I’m remembering right" when recalling dates; will quote approximate ranges and then look for confirmation
emotional_tendencies: calmer when there is a plan and timeline
profile.decision_making_style: Research-first and comparison-based; prefers to see options side-by-side (tax impact, cash flow, downside) before agreeing
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously trusting; open to advice but expects competence and transparency, especially around taxes and implementation
internal_biases: overconfidence in ability to manage complexity
loss aversion framed as "not wanting to give money to taxes unnecessarily"
anchoring to prior strategies that worked
hidden_concerns: worries about sustaining lifestyle without feeling like they are "drawing down too fast"
concerned about tax bracket management and surprises
quietly concerned about health habits affecting long-term planning but may deflect with humor

profile.name: Jennifer Smith
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; supports decisions and focuses on day-to-day cash flow and peace of mind
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate — understands budgeting and basic retirement concepts, but prefers plain-English explanations over technical tax/investment jargon
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate-leaning conservative in practice; comfortable with diversified investing but becomes protective when markets are volatile and prefers keeping a noticeable cash buffer
profile.communication_style: Warm and conversational; uses fillers like "um" and "you know"; often says "I’m not totally sure" about exact numbers; asks clarifying questions and summarizes back what she heard to confirm
emotional_tendencies: anxious during uncertainty (market swings, big changes)
relieved by simple rules and guardrails
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-driven; prefers to talk it through, sleep on it, and then agree together—rarely pushes for immediate action
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Generally trusting once rapport is established; values patience and clear explanations more than technical depth
internal_biases: availability bias (recent headlines influence comfort level)
status quo bias (prefers minimal changes unless clearly beneficial)
hidden_concerns: worries about having enough liquid cash for emergencies and home-related surprises
concerned about complexity and "too many accounts" even if she can’t name them precisely
doesn’t want financial decisions to create tension in the relationship
